title: Thelypteris lushuiensis
taxonomy: Thelypteris lushuiensis is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is classified under the phylum Pteridophyta, kingdom Plantae, and is further divided into the order Polypodiales, and then the class Polypodiopsida. Within the class Polypodiopsida, it is placed under the subclass Polypodiidae. At the family level, it is categorized as a member of the Thelypteridaceae family, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or wood ferns. The species is also known to be a part of the genus Thelypteris, which is a genus of deciduous and evergreen ferns.

intro: Thelypteris lushuiensis, also known as the Lushui fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Asia and is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris lushuiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris lushuiensis is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, or used as a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
